summary: Current HEAD equals origin/main. Merged PR #3288 added a source-destroying sandbox worktree-archive replay, selector coverage, CI wiring, a replay manifest, and developer commands, but it explicitly did not implement the required production-composed managed Codex cold-resume journey. The current repository still lacks that primary Temporal/UserWorkflow/AgentRun recovery journey, the required continuation-phase journeys, the adversarial/fault-injection matrix, and the dedicated replay corpus. No residual issue-content truncation remains.

requirements.description: Hermetic source-destroying archive capture/restore coverage exists for the production archive and artifact boundary, with manifest integrity and restore idempotency evidence.

Primary cold Resume journey composes the Temporal test server, real UserWorkflow recovery creation, real AgentRun, real managed-agent adapter/run store, managed capture/restore activities, Step Execution ledger, and boundary-aware continuation.

The primary journey proves source session/container/runtime/workspace destruction, a distinct destination agent-run identity and typed locator, durable-only recovery, preserved prepare provenance, exactly-once recovered implement, and downstream verify ordering.
Workspace fidelity covers and verifies all required file types, modes, symlinks, exclusions, Git base commit, and canonical Git status digest through the managed recovery journey.
Composed journeys cover before_execution, after_execution, after_gate, before_publication, and before_recovery_restoration/restore-retry continuation semantics.

Permanent adversarial and fault-injection coverage proves stable authority, artifact-integrity, filesystem-safety, Git-consistency, capability, side-effect, replay, and idempotent-reconciliation failures.

A deterministic dedicated cold-resume replay corpus records the required identities, phase/capability data, artifact digests, routes, invocation counts, lineage, terminal codes, and incident references.
Test selection emits reliability_journey and over-selects relevant backend/runtime/checkpoint/recovery/worker/CI paths.

Required hermetic CI runs the reliability journey with bounded timeout, uploads bounded diagnostics on failure, runs for main/schedule/manual events, and the always-running summary rejects an unsuccessful selected job.
Developer documentation provides canonical full-suite, selector, and fixture-specific replay commands and accurately states the production boundary still missing.
